symptoms.symptom_name: Severe orthostatic near-syncope (dizziness and vision blackout on standing)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cortisol deficiency causes impaired vascular tone and orthostatic hypotension.
Near-syncope with inability to maintain posture signals haemodynamic decompensation.
Cortisol and thyroid deficiency produce profound asthenia; non-specific alone but severe here.
TSH deficiency from pituitary failure causes central hypothyroidism with cold intolerance.
Cold intolerance fits hypothyroidism of any cause; does not by itself localise the lesion.
Gonadotropin deficiency from pituitary failure causes secondary amenorrhoea.
Amenorrhoea also occurs in anorexia; needs the other pituitary features to differentiate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Loss of axillary and pubic hair

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loss of androgen-dependent (adrenal + gonadal) hair reflects combined ACTH and gonadotropin deficiency.
Frank loss of axillary/pubic hair points to organic endocrine failure rather than an eating disorder.
Primary adrenal insufficiency causes ACTH-driven hyperpigmentation; pale skin argues against it and toward a secondary (pituitary) cause.
Low ACTH means no hyperpigmentation; pallor is characteristic of pituitary (secondary) adrenal failure.
Cortisol deficiency impairs gluconeogenesis, causing fasting hypoglycaemia with adrenergic/neuroglycopenic symptoms.
Chronic multi-axis hormone deficiency contributes to weight loss; non-specific.
Severe postpartum haemorrhage is the classic cause of ischaemic anterior pituitary necrosis (Sheehan syndrome).

symptoms.symptom_name: Measured severe orthostatic hypotension (BP drop on standing)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective postural blood-pressure drop confirms haemodynamic decompensation — a sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers this from the reported near-syncope, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Objective global muscle weakness (power 3/5 on examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-graded weakness — sealed examination finding not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Low-normal, consistent with the reported recurrent hypoglycaemic episodes from cortisol deficiency
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Hypoglycaemia missed as a contributor to the confusional episodes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Low-normal glucose with symptomatic hypoglycaemic episodes fits cortisol deficiency; clinician-obtained at the visit and sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Anterior pituitary hormone panel (cortisol/ACTH, TSH/free T4, LH/FSH, prolactin, IGF-1)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Formal anterior pituitary hormone profile + electrolytes
investigation_details.expected_result: Multiple low anterior pituitary hormones with low target-gland outputs, confirming panhypopituitarism (pending hospital assay)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis and hormone-axis mapping not confirmed; replacement cannot be correctly sequenced

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Formal confirmation is a hospital assay — never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Cold intolerance + amenorrhoea + hair loss + pale skin on a background of severe postpartum haemorrhage is the classic multi-axis pituitary-failure constellation.
Severe orthostatic near-syncope, profound weakness and hypoglycaemic episodes together signal an emergency requiring immediate escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency hospital bloods — cortisol, ACTH, electrolytes (sodium), glucose
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms adrenal crisis and guides resuscitation; must be obtained in hospital and must NOT delay treatment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Anterior pituitary hormone profile (TSH/free T4, LH/FSH, prolactin, IGF-1)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Maps the deficient axes for correctly sequenced hormone replacement (glucocorticoid before thyroxine).
Assesses the pituitary (empty sella / infarction of Sheehan syndrome) once stabilised.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: AI Doctor independently ordering/awaiting outpatient labs before escalating
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: In a suspected adrenal crisis, arranging routine outpatient tests instead of emergency transfer is a dangerous delay — escalate first.
